diagrams.title: Software Quality Framework
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between Quality Planning, Quality Assurance, and Quality Control in project management
elements: Quality Planning - Define standards and objectives
Quality Assurance - Process audit and prevention
Quality Control - Product inspection and testing
Feedback loops to improve all phases
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand the three-pillar approach to software quality management and how they interconnect in project delivery

diagrams.title: Cost of Quality Model
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the four components of Cost of Quality with relative investment priorities
elements: Arrow showing: invest more in prevention to reduce failure costs
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates why organizations should prioritize prevention over detection, showing that failure costs are exponentially higher than prevention costs

diagrams.title: ISO 9126 Quality Characteristics
diagrams.description: Six-attribute quality model with sub-characteristics for software product evaluation
diagrams.purpose: Provides a structured framework for defining, measuring, and evaluating software quality attributes in projects
